name: ComponentDefinition
summary: The ComponentDefinition class is used to define the contents for a SketchUp component. Components are a collection of entities that can be instanced and reused multiple times throughout a model. For example, you could draw a chair once, turn it into a component, and then use 6 instances of it to surround a table. Edits to the original “definition” will then propagate across all of its instances. The ComponentDefinition class contains the global entities and settings for each definition. See the ComponentInstance class for how each copy is defined. Starting from SketchUp 2018+, the ComponentDefinition class contains a new default attribute dictionary named “SU_DefinitionSet” with default keys named named “Price”, “Size”, “Url”. See the Help article for more information. The dictionary cannot be deleted via ruby and an ArgumentError will be raised. The key/value pairs in the dictionary can be deleted safely.

methods.name: <=>
methods.summary: The <=> method is used to compare two ComponentDefinition objects for sorting.
methods.remarks: The <=> method is used to compare two ComponentDefinition objects for sorting. The comparison is done based on the component name.

params.name: compdef2
params.doc: The second component definition in the comparison.

returns.doc: a -1 if component1 is less then component2. A 1 if component1 greater than component2

methods.name: add_classification
methods.summary: The add_classification method is used to add a given classification to the component.
methods.remarks: The add_classification method is used to add a given classification to the component. Note that you cannot classify image definitions.

params.name: schema_name
params.doc: a String - Schema name to add

params.name: schema_type
params.doc: a String - Schema type to add

returns.doc: true if the classification succeeds. Otherwise false.

methods.name: add_observer
methods.summary: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the current object.
methods.remarks: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the current object.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: behavior
methods.summary: The behavior method is used to retrieve the Behavior object associated with a component definition.
methods.remarks: The behavior method is used to retrieve the Behavior object associated with a component definition.

methods.name: count_instances
methods.summary: The count_instances method is used to count the number of unique component instances in a model using this component definition.
methods.remarks: The count_instances method is used to count the number of unique component instances in a model using this component definition. This does not represent the total number of instances placed in the model as it doesn't take into account instances inside unused definitions.

returns.doc: the number of component instances of this component definition (if successful)

methods.name: count_used_instances
methods.summary: The count_used_instances method is used to count the total number of component instances in a model using this component definition.
methods.remarks: The count_used_instances method is used to count the total number of component instances in a model using this component definition. This method takes into account the full hierarchy of the model.

returns.doc: the number of component instances of this component definition (if successful)

methods.name: entities
methods.summary: The entities method retrieves a collection of all the entities in the component definition
methods.remarks: The entities method retrieves a collection of all the entities in the component definition

methods.name: get_classification_value
methods.summary: The get_classification_value method is used to retrieve the value from a classification attribute given a key path.
methods.remarks: The get_classification_value method is used to retrieve the value from a classification attribute given a key path.

params.name: path
params.doc: An array composed of the key path to the value.

returns.doc: a Ruby object if successful, nil otherwise.

methods.name: group?
methods.summary: The group? method is used to determine if this component definition is used to hold the elements of a group.
methods.remarks: The group? method is used to determine if this component definition is used to hold the elements of a group.

methods.name: guid
methods.summary: The guid method is used to retrieve the unique identifier of this component definition.
methods.remarks: The guid method is used to retrieve the unique identifier of this component definition. The guid changes after the component definition is modified and the component edit is exited.

methods.name: hidden?
methods.summary: The #hidden? method is used to determine if this component definition is hidden in the component browser.
methods.remarks: The #hidden? method is used to determine if this component definition is hidden in the component browser. This is based on how its instances are placed in the model hierarchy. For more details, see this article. In addition, component definitions used by Groups and Images are always hidden in the Component Browser. See #group? and #image?.

methods.name: image?
methods.summary: The image? method is used to determine if this component definition is used to define an image.
methods.remarks: The image? method is used to determine if this component definition is used to define an image.

methods.name: instances
methods.summary: The instances method is used to return any array of ComponentInstancesfor this ComponentDefinition.
methods.remarks: The instances method is used to return any array of ComponentInstancesfor this ComponentDefinition.

methods.name: internal?
methods.summary: The internal? method is used to determine if the component definition is internal to the Component Browser
methods.remarks: The internal? method is used to determine if the component definition is internal to the Component Browser

methods.name: invalidate_bounds
methods.summary: Invalidates the bounding box of your definition.
methods.remarks: Invalidates the bounding box of your definition. This command forces the update of the bounding box of definition while inside an operation. See Model.start_operation for how to start an operation. This method is useful if you make changes to your geometry using the Ruby API and then need to know your bounding box size. This method forces SketchUp to recalculate the definition's bounding box when you choose.

methods.name: live_component?
methods.summary: Note: These components are parametrically generated and API users should not modify them.
methods.remarks: Note: These components are parametrically generated and API users should not modify them. The #live_component? method is used to identify Live Components and sub-definitions of Live Components.

methods.name: load_time
methods.summary: The #load_time method gets the load time of the component definition.
methods.remarks: The #load_time method gets the load time of the component definition. For an internal component definition, this is the time that it was created. For an external component definition, this is the time that it was added to the model.

methods.name: path
methods.summary: The path method is used to retrieve the path where the component was loaded.
methods.remarks: The path method is used to retrieve the path where the component was loaded.

returns.doc: Returns empty string if it is an internal component.

methods.name: refresh_thumbnail
methods.summary: The refresh_thumbnail method is used to force SketchUp to regenerate the thumbnail image that appears in the component browser.
methods.remarks: The refresh_thumbnail method is used to force SketchUp to regenerate the thumbnail image that appears in the component browser. This is useful if you've used the API to change the geometry of your component and would like the thumbnail to match.

methods.name: remove_classification
methods.summary: The remove_classification method is used to remove a given classification from the component.
methods.remarks: The remove_classification method is used to remove a given classification from the component. Note that you cannot classify image definitions.

params.name: schema_type
params.doc: Schema type to remove. If not provided or an empty string, the currently applied schema type for the given schema name will be removed.

returns.doc: true if the removal succeeds. Otherwise false.

methods.name: remove_observer
methods.summary: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.
methods.remarks: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: save_as
methods.summary: The #save_as method is used to save your definition as a SketchUp file at the specified file destination.
methods.remarks: The #save_as method is used to save your definition as a SketchUp file at the specified file destination. Use this method when the user has chosen a path. If you want to “silently” save out the definition, without changing the path it is associated with, use #save_copy instead.

methods.name: save_copy
methods.summary: The #save_copy method is used to save your definition as a SketchUp file without changing the file path it is already associated with.
methods.remarks: The #save_copy method is used to save your definition as a SketchUp file without changing the file path it is already associated with. This can be used to save out to a temporary file used by some other process, without having the temporary path permanentely written to the SketchUp model.

methods.name: save_thumbnail
methods.summary: Saves a component thumbnail image.
methods.remarks: Saves a component thumbnail image. The image format is specified by the file extension of filePath. Supported image formats are bmp, jpg, png, tif, pct, and gif.

methods.name: set_classification_value
methods.summary: The set_classification_value method is used to set the value of a classification attribute given a key path.
methods.remarks: The set_classification_value method is used to set the value of a classification attribute given a key path.

params.name: path
params.doc: An array composed of the key path to the value.

params.name: value
params.doc: A value valid for that specific attribute.

returns.doc: true if the path was valid, false otherwise.

throws.when: when trying to set the value of “choice” attributes.
if the attributes being set are corrupt.
when trying to set a value that is not valid for the attribute.

properties.summary: The == method is used to test if two ComponentDefinition objects are the same (based on their address in memory).
properties.remarks: The == method is used to test if two ComponentDefinition objects are the same (based on their address in memory).

properties.name: description
properties.summary: The description method is used to retrieve the description of the component definition.
properties.remarks: The description method is used to retrieve the description of the component definition. Component definitions do not have a description, by default.

properties.name: insertion_point
properties.remarks: Deprecated. SketchUp 2020.0 removed the insertion point feature. The getter will always return the origin point and the setter becomes a no-op. The insertion_point method is used to retrieve the Point3d object where the component was inserted.

properties.name: name
properties.summary: The name method retrieves the name of the component definition.
properties.remarks: The name method retrieves the name of the component definition.

properties.name: thumbnail_camera
properties.summary: The #thumbnail_camera method is used to retrieve a camera representing the thumbnail associated with the component definition.
properties.remarks: The #thumbnail_camera method is used to retrieve a camera representing the thumbnail associated with the component definition.
